meta.professor_methodology: Emphasis on right-hand rule and the vector nature of magnetic forces
meta.core_formula: F = qv × B, F = IL × B, r = mv/qB
meta.description: Lorentz force, motion of charged particles in magnetic fields, and magnetic field sources

patterns.name: Circular Motion in B-Fields
patterns.recognition_signal: Charged particle moving perpendicular to uniform magnetic field
patterns.logic: Magnetic force provides centripetal force for circular motion

efficiency_trick.name: Period Independence
steps: Remember T = 2πm/qB is independent of velocity

pitfalls.name: Direction errors
pitfalls.description: Use right-hand rule for positive; reverse for negative charges

pitfalls.name: Velocity dependence confusion
pitfalls.description: Radius depends on v, but period does not

worked_example.title: Proton in Magnetic Field
worked_example.problem_latex: Proton with v = 10⁶ m/s enters B = 0.1 T perpendicular to field. Find radius.

professor_tips: Always use the right-hand rule to check direction before calculating
Magnetic force does NO work (always perpendicular to velocity)
Period is independent of velocity - this is crucial for cyclotrons
For velocity selectors, E and B fields are perpendicular and forces balance

confidence_checklist: I can apply the Lorentz force law
I can calculate radius of circular motion in uniform B-field
I understand that magnetic force does no work
I can apply the right-hand rule correctly
I understand velocity selectors and mass spectrometers
